pi_items.source: Web search confirmed DreamPlan Home Design Software by NCH Software uses .ddp (not .dpp) file extension. Multiple validated_pi.json files from other DreamPlan tasks in this same environment (change_room_ceiling_height, change_wall_paint_color, construct_freestanding_carport) document this error with sources: https://www.file-extensions.org/ddp-file-extension-dreamplan-home-design-software-project, https://fileinfo.com/extension/ddp, https://filext.com/file-extension/DDP

Cannot verify - the export_result.ps1 script referenced in task.json post_task hook is missing from the repository, making it impossible to determine the actual directory where files are saved. Other DreamPlan tasks in this environment use different paths: 'Documents\DreamPlan Projects' (create_backyard_patio, add_backyard_gazebo) and 'Documents\DreamPlan' (change_wall_paint_color). NCH Software help documentation states 'The default location for saving projects is the Documents folder' but does not specify the subdirectory structure.

Verified through setup_dreamplan.ps1 installation script and env.json description. DreamPlan executable is at C:\Program Files (x86)\NCH Software\DreamPlan\dreamplan.exe (confirmed in setup_dreamplan.ps1 lines 23-26, 78-81). DreamPlan by NCH Software is the official application name per https://www.nchsoftware.com/design/index.html

pi_items.verified_value: DreamPlan Home Design v9.28 by NCH Software
pi_items.source: Verified through evidence_docs/README.md line 4: 'Application: DreamPlan Home Design v9.28 by NCH Software (free/unlicensed version)'. Installation script setup_dreamplan.ps1 downloads from https://www.nchsoftware.com/design/designsetup.exe

Verified through evidence_docs/README.md line 6 and lines 42-49 which confirm all tasks use Contemporary House sample project. setup_dreamplan.ps1 lines 233-241 and task_utils.ps1 Launch-DreamPlanWithSample function (lines 586-629) explicitly load Contemporary House.

Cannot verify bay window support in DreamPlan. Web searches and NCH Software documentation confirm DreamPlan has a windows library (https://help.nchsoftware.com/help/en/dreamplan/win/adding_windowsanddoors.html) and supports adding windows from a catalog, but specific window types are not enumerated in public documentation. Web searches for 'DreamPlan bay window' did not return specific documentation confirming bay windows are available as a standard library element.
Verified through env.json line 4: 'base': 'windows-11'. Evidence_docs/README.md line 5 confirms 'Base image: windows-11 (UEFI/OVMF, 8GB RAM)'
Verified through env.json line 2: 'id': 'dreamplan_home_design_software_env@0.1'. Also confirmed in task.json line 4.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses NCH DreamPlan Home Design Software v9.28 (real, non-synthetic application) running on Windows 11. It targets the Contemporary House sample project. The task requires replacing a standard window with a bay window from the Windows library and saving the modified project. However, the metadata contains a critical filename extension error: the task specifies 'RenovatedBayWindow.dpp' but DreamPlan Home Design Software uses the .ddp file extension for project files, not .dpp. The correct filename should be 'RenovatedBayWindow.ddp'. The expected save directory 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\DreamPlan' cannot be verified because the export_result.ps1 script (referenced in task hooks) is missing from the repository. The application name 'dreamplan' is correct. Bay window support in DreamPlan could not be verified from public documentation.
